description_long: This object is a compact, modern rice cooker with a predominantly black exterior and a slightly rounded cubic form. The top surface features a control panel area, likely with buttons and a display, set in a contrasting color, possibly dark red or brown. The front face appears to have a circular button or latch, suggesting a push-to-open mechanism for the lid. The body is smooth and glossy, with subtle seams indicating the separation between the lid and the main body. The overall design is minimalistic, with no visible handles or protrusions, and the appliance sits flat on its base. The rice cooker is likely constructed from hard plastic with internal metal components for heating and cooking.
description: A compact, glossy black rice cooker with a minimalistic design and a control panel on top. The front features a circular button or latch, and the body is smooth with subtle seams.
description_view_0: The rice cooker is seen from a front-side angle, showing the control panel on top and the side curvature of the body.
description_view_1: This direct front view highlights the circular button or latch and the flat, glossy front surface.
description_view_2: The rear-side angle reveals the back curvature and the top control panel, with no visible handles or cords.
description_view_3: A direct rear view shows the back of the rice cooker, with a smooth, featureless surface and the top control panel just visible.
attribute_control_panel_layout: The control panel is located on the top surface, rectangular in shape, and appears to have several buttons and possibly a small display screen. The buttons are likely arranged in a row or grid, but specific labels or icons are not visible due to image darkness.
attribute_lid_design: The lid is integrated into the top of the body, slightly domed, and likely hinged at the rear. It appears to open upward and may have a push-button release at the front. No visible vents or windows are present.
